---
title: "Product Brief"
subtitle: "What the Commercial Intelligence Lab is — in one read"
---

::: {.page-context}
::: {.pc-crumb}
Start Here <span class="sep">›</span> Product Brief
:::
::: {.pc-supports}
**Audience:** investors, operators, and anyone who wants the whole picture without the machinery.
:::
::: {.pc-row}
<span class="chip chip-grade-c no-dot">v0.1 · Hypothesis stage</span>
<span class="pc-next">[See the Decisions](../decisions/index.qmd)</span>
:::
:::

## The one-paragraph version

We took the Sigmoid Analytica CRM proposal and turned it from a slide-deck argument into a **living
decision system** — a site where commercial strategy is broken into concrete decisions, scored with
transparent, adjustable frameworks, and held to an explicit standard for what counts as evidence. It
runs on real code with an automated test suite, so every number can be re-run and audited. Crucially,
it is **honest about what it does not yet know**: nothing is presented as a validated fact.

## Why it is built this way

A proposal is an argument made *before* the evidence is in. If we treat it as truth, we bake in
untested assumptions about market, positioning, and pricing. So the project is governed by one rule
set — the [Commercial Intelligence Specification (CIS)](../cis/commercial-intelligence-specification.md) —
that treats the proposal as **Hypothesis v0.1** and forces every recommendation through a fixed
lifecycle before it can claim authority. Every claim carries a **confidence grade**: D (asserted) → C
(illustrative) → B (substantiated) → A (validated). Today everything is C or D, by design.

## The three decisions the whole thing exists to make

| Decision | Question | Status |
|---|---|---|
| [Market Entry](../decisions/market-entry.qmd) | Which segment should we target first for the CRM? | Hypothesis (C) |
| [Positioning](../decisions/positioning.qmd) | CRM, Revenue Operations, or AI Readiness Platform? | Hypothesis (C) |
| [GTM Evidence Gate](../decisions/gtm-evidence-gate.qmd) | What evidence is required before we commit to GTM? | Gate defined (D) |

## What is in it — the surfaces

| Surface | What it is |
|---|---|
| [Decisions](../decisions/index.qmd) | The three strategic decisions, each linked to its evidence, framework, and dashboard |
| [Evidence](../evidence/index.qmd) | The register of claims, their confidence, and what still needs proving |
| [Frameworks](../frameworks/index.qmd) | Five transparent scoring models with editable weights |
| [Dashboards](../dashboards/index.qmd) | Three interactive tools that recompute from the data |
| [Working Paper](../working-paper/index.qmd) | The long-form discussion behind each decision |
| [Governance](../governance/index.qmd) | The standard (CIS), decision records, and testing standard |

## The engine (why the numbers are trustworthy)

- All scoring/validation/evidence logic lives once in `src/`; dashboards import it, they do not
  reinvent it.
- **Weights live in YAML, not code** — change a weight, re-render, the ranking moves.
- **53 automated tests** guard every calculation. A red suite is a hard stop.
- All sample data is **synthetic** and labelled as such.

## Honest status

v0.1, hypothesis stage. No segment, positioning, or GTM strategy is recommended yet. The pivotal next
experiment is the **AI-readiness pilot**. See [Current Status](current-status.qmd) for the live
picture and [How to Use This Lab](how-to-use.qmd) to start working with it.
